basics.name: Andrew Wdzieczkowski
basics.label: Student
basics.summary: I am studying Computer Engineering for a B.S. in Computer and Electrical Engineering at the University of Hawaii at Manoa. My expected graduation is Fall 2026.

work.summary: Assisted with various agricultural tasks and implemented technological solutions to improve efficiency.
highlights: Assisted with planting and harvesting crops year-round.
Supplied irrigation and fertilizer to crops on a weekly basis.
Scouted crops to inspect for pests, weeds, diseases, and deficiencies.
Developed Microsoft apps to make data tracking more efficient.
Automated the irrigation system with DXM configuration and Ignition Perspective.

work.summary: Maintained high-volume kitchen operations by strictly adhering to food safety protocols and quality standards.
highlights: Ensured quality and health standards in food preparation.
Expanded knowledge of all food preparation areas.
Gained strong knowledge and understanding of entrees and ingredients required.
Maintained kitchen cleanliness standards in accordance with state regulations.

volunteer.summary: Provided dedicated animal husbandry and behavioral support while collaborating with shelter staff to ensure a high standard of care.
highlights: Assisted with providing care for animals at the shelter.
Demonstrated enthusiasm and cheerfulness with animals and staff.
Proactively learned and developed new specialized skills.
Able to communicate effectively with peers and animals.
Gained expertise in how to interact with animals with different behaviors.

awards.title: Phi Theta Kappa Honor Society
awards.summary: Honor society offers membership to students who have completed 12 credits and have a cumulative GPA of 3.5 or higher.
